title: Lasiandra oleifolia
taxonomy: Lasiandra oleifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Lasiandra, which is a group of plants native to Central and South America. Lasiandra oleifolia is further classified as a member of the section Lasiandra, which is characterized by its unique leaf and flower morphology. The species is often found growing in humid and subtropical environments. Within the Rubiaceae family, Lasiandra oleifolia is classified as a member of the order Gentianales.

The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, Lasiandra oleifolia is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
